Cheyenne|WY.climateNote: Cheyenne sees some of the coldest temperatures in the lower 48. Dual-fuel systems (heat pump + gas furnace backup) are gaining popularity for their efficiency in moderate cold while maintaining gas reliability for extreme days.
Cheyenne|WY.systemTip: In Cheyenne's extreme cold, a gas furnace rated 96%+ AFUE is the safest choice. Standard heat pumps lose efficiency below 20°F, though cold-climate heat pumps now work down to -15°F.
Cheyenne|WY.seasonNote: Don't gamble on an aging furnace in Cheyenne's winters. If your system is 15+ years old, schedule a replacement in early fall when you can plan rather than panic.
Cheyenne|WY.localInsight: In Cheyenne, a furnace failure in January can be dangerous. Keep an emergency plan: know a reliable HVAC company, consider a backup heat source, and don't ignore warning signs like strange noises or uneven heating.
